jokes.text: What are we gonna watch, a silent movie from your childhood?
jokes.setup: Roland assumes Johnny is watching a movie for entertainment
jokes.punchline: Age-based insult about silent movies from Johnny's 'childhood'
jokes.explanation: Classic Roland tactlessness, economically delivered but predictable old-age humor

jokes.text: it's clear from the stain on your top that things have gotten just a little lax around here
jokes.setup: Johnny explaining workplace etiquette video necessity
jokes.punchline: Pointing out Roland's immediate embodiment of the problem
jokes.explanation: Good character-driven humor with Johnny's exasperation and immediate evidence of his point

jokes.text: Oh wow, this looks like the videos Joce and I used to make.
jokes.setup: Roland commenting on professional workplace video
jokes.punchline: Unintended implication about homemade videos with his wife
jokes.explanation: Perfect Roland overshare creating uncomfortable implications - exactly in character

jokes.text: Excuse me, Emilio! Pause. That kind of physical touch is inapprop-ri-rate.
jokes.setup: Moira in workplace etiquette video correcting behavior
jokes.punchline: Her signature pretentious mispronunciation of 'inappropriate'
jokes.explanation: Quintessential Moira word mangling - perfectly integrated character comedy with great quotability

jokes.text: And now they're gonna come and do it again!
jokes.setup: Watching workplace training video with rewind/replay
jokes.punchline: Roland's inappropriate excitement about replaying the scene
jokes.explanation: Roland's inappropriate enthusiasm is in character but the joke itself is straightforward

jokes.text: A double-cassette of the 'Best Body Swaps of Sunrise Bay', Season 21
jokes.setup: Customer requesting video at Rose Video
jokes.punchline: Absurdly specific soap opera compilation request
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly specific absurdity that feels perfectly authentic to a small-town video store

jokes.text: I've been asked to retrieve it for a customer with wonderful taste.
jokes.setup: Retrieving absurd soap opera compilation
jokes.punchline: Stevie's deadpan praise for terrible taste
jokes.explanation: Perfect Stevie deadpan delivery with economical irony - exactly her character

jokes.text: Perhaps you'd like to meet outside of work for a dinner at my place?
jokes.setup: Workplace etiquette training video demonstration
jokes.punchline: Moira's inappropriate workplace advance in the 'educational' video
jokes.explanation: The irony of inappropriate behavior in an etiquette video is solid character comedy

jokes.text: Fast forward! - Oh! - Jesus.
jokes.setup: Everyone watching Moira's inappropriate advance in training video
jokes.punchline: Johnny's panicked attempt to skip past his wife's bad acting
jokes.explanation: Good reaction comedy with Johnny's embarrassment coming through economically

jokes.text: Uh oh, ladies! Somebody better call Poison Control, because tonight's gonna be... ♪ Nothin' but a good time! ♪
jokes.setup: Jocelyn excited about Poison concert
jokes.punchline: Elaborate pun connecting band name to poison control hotline
jokes.explanation: Excellent elaborate pun that perfectly captures Jocelyn's enthusiastic personality

jokes.text: Because when Rikki Rockett makes direct eye contact with you during 'Talk Dirty to Me', you are gonna want to talk dirty back!
jokes.setup: Jocelyn explaining why they need to rehearse for the concert
jokes.punchline: Inappropriate sexual fantasy about band member interaction
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jocelyn inappropriateness with specific rock band knowledge showing character depth

jokes.text: Looks like they double-booked Poison with The Doodlebops.
jokes.setup: Explaining why Poison concert was cancelled
jokes.punchline: Absurd pairing of hair metal band with children's musical group
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly absurd contrast between adult metal and kids' entertainment that feels authentic to small-town venue booking

jokes.text: I didn't pump for four hours straight to get on a chartered bus to go to the end of the street and back!
jokes.setup: Jocelyn's disappointment about cancelled concert
jokes.punchline: TMI about breast pumping preparation for short trip
jokes.explanation: Classic Jocelyn overshare with specific commitment details that emphasize the anticlimax

jokes.text: No, but I can ask George to burn something on the grill.
jokes.setup: Jocelyn asking if cafe has smoke machine like rock concert
jokes.punchline: Twyla's practical alternative using cooking accidents
jokes.explanation: Excellent Twyla deadpan with creative problem-solving that's both practical and absurd

jokes.text: When Condi Rice, and Sharon Stone and I, used to make our annual casino sojourn to Ho Chi Minh City, well, let's just say, toi khong bao gio thua.
jokes.setup: Moira claiming gambling experience
jokes.punchline: Absurd celebrity name-drops plus Vietnamese phrase meaning 'I never lose'
jokes.explanation: Peak Moira absurdity with specific cultural references and foreign language showing off - perfectly in character

jokes.text: And this is the only casino I haven't been banned from as an unaccompanied minor.
jokes.setup: Discussion about going to casino
jokes.punchline: Casual admission of childhood gambling problem
jokes.explanation: Perfect Alexis dark backstory revelation delivered casually - great character integration with economy

jokes.text: Sorry, I'm just distracted, there's a guy over there currently flirting with my boyfriend.
jokes.setup: David distracted during business meeting
jokes.punchline: Immediate acknowledgment of relationship threat
jokes.explanation: David's possessive observation sets up episode's romantic tension competently

jokes.text: - Poor thing. - I know. No, I meant you, David.
jokes.setup: Alexis seemingly sympathizing with someone
jokes.punchline: Clarifying she meant David, not Patrick
jokes.explanation: Clean misdirection joke with good sibling dynamic - economical structure

jokes.text: Hm, you planning on doing some journaling?
jokes.setup: Customer asking for pen and paper
jokes.punchline: David's sarcastic assumption about innocent request
jokes.explanation: David's suspicious nature shown through sarcastic customer service

jokes.text: Just when I thought it was impossible to find a thirty-something named Ken!
jokes.setup: Learning customer's name is Ken
jokes.punchline: Sarcastic amazement at finding such a 'generic' name
jokes.explanation: Good observational humor about generic names with David's snobby delivery

jokes.text: Get fucked Alexis!
jokes.setup: Alexis making innocent comment about Ken's age
jokes.punchline: David's disproportionate hostile reaction
jokes.explanation: Sharp escalation showing David's jealous spiral through economical outburst

jokes.text: I mean, I think it's funny 'cause when he first walked in here I was gonna help him, so to think it could've been me enjoying Ken.
jokes.setup: Alexis reflecting on the customer interaction
jokes.punchline: Her narcissistic imagining of alternate scenario
jokes.explanation: Classic Alexis self-centeredness turning situation to be about her

jokes.text: Do you mean the woman who wanted to know when the soap would be back in stock? Who came in here with her husband and kids?
jokes.setup: Patrick claiming woman gave him her number
jokes.punchline: David's reality check about the obviously innocent interaction
jokes.explanation: Good deflation of Patrick's delusional claim with specific details

jokes.text: Hasn't stopped me before.
jokes.setup: David pointing out woman was married with kids
jokes.punchline: Patrick's deadpan admission of homewrecking history
jokes.explanation: Perfectly economical dark humor - maximum impact with minimal words, subverts Patrick's nice guy image

jokes.text: Oh! Oh! Oh my God, Mr. Rose! Oh!
jokes.setup: Johnny walking in on Stevie
jokes.punchline: Stevie's panicked reaction to being caught taking intimate photos
jokes.explanation: Standard awkward encounter setup but effective for the cringe comedy

jokes.text: Taking a personal photo... For my doctor.
jokes.setup: Stevie trying to explain why she was taking photos
jokes.punchline: Transparent lie about medical necessity
jokes.explanation: Classic awkward excuse with Stevie's deadpan delivery making it funnier

jokes.text: Right, well, it was, it was a medical uh, photo, and, and uh... and you what? You text or email this right back to your doctor, I mean, what a-what an age we live in!
jokes.setup: Johnny trying to process Stevie's excuse
jokes.punchline: His awkward attempt to normalize the situation with technology marvel
jokes.explanation: Johnny's characteristic rambling attempt to make awkward situation less awkward makes it worse

jokes.text: I wish we had gotten to the second tape in the workplace etiquette series, that had a whole chunk on Polaroids.
jokes.setup: Awkward photo situation with Stevie
jokes.punchline: Johnny wishing workplace training covered this specific scenario
jokes.explanation: Excellent callback that connects to opening scene while maintaining Johnny's character consistency

jokes.text: just uh, maybe try and find a room with a locked door?
jokes.setup: Johnny giving Stevie advice about private moments
jokes.punchline: His practical but still awkward guidance
jokes.explanation: Johnny trying to be helpful while maintaining dignity - competent character comedy

jokes.text: You know what? I was going to change and then I thought, no, if my babysitter can show up wearing a crop top the size of a bib, I can wear this to a casino!
jokes.setup: Jocelyn explaining her outfit choice for casino
jokes.punchline: Comparing her dress to babysitter's inappropriate attire
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Jocelyn's justification logic with specific details

jokes.text: If the bus is a-rockin', don't come a-knockin'! Wooo! Yeah!
jokes.setup: Getting on bus for casino night
jokes.punchline: Inappropriate sexual reference for ladies' night out
jokes.explanation: Predictable innuendo but perfectly in character for Jocelyn's inappropriate enthusiasm

jokes.text: The pills came from Condi, so if anyone asks, you found them on the floor.
jokes.setup: Moira bringing casino essentials including caffeine pills
jokes.punchline: Casual reference to getting drugs from Condoleezza Rice with cover story
jokes.explanation: Peak Moira absurdity - celebrity name-dropping combined with criminal conspiracy delivered casually. Highly quotable.

jokes.text: this afternoon I made some... magic brownies!
jokes.setup: Twyla offering to contribute refreshments
jokes.punchline: Sweet Twyla casually mentioning marijuana edibles
jokes.explanation: Great character subversion - innocent Twyla casually offering drugs contrasts perfectly with her personality

jokes.text: this afternoon I made some... magic brownies!
jokes.setup: Twyla offering supplies for the casino trip
jokes.punchline: Sweet Twyla casually offering marijuana brownies
jokes.explanation: Great character subversion - innocent Twyla offering drugs. The pause before 'magic brownies' builds anticipation.

jokes.text: Oh Moira, we don't expect you to have one. One? At least one.
jokes.setup: Jocelyn assuming Moira won't partake in marijuana brownies
jokes.punchline: Moira's enthusiastic correction about quantity
jokes.explanation: Perfect subversion of expectations about prissy Moira, delivered with great economy and timing

jokes.text: Best case scenario, you realize how good you have it with me. Worst case scenario, you realize how good you have it with me.
jokes.setup: David explaining why Patrick should date Ken
jokes.punchline: His supreme confidence that both outcomes lead to the same conclusion
jokes.explanation: Excellent structure with David's characteristic arrogance delivered perfectly - highly quotable confidence

jokes.text: Mr. Rose saw me topless. - Oh! Oh! - No! No!
jokes.setup: David asking what happened earlier
jokes.punchline: Stevie's blunt revelation causing shocked reactions
jokes.explanation: Efficient cringe comedy with good reaction beats, though setup is predictable

jokes.text: Well, I did just think of one thing. - Please don't! - It's more of a limerick.
jokes.setup: Roland saying he thought of something to say about situation
jokes.punchline: His intention to compose inappropriate poetry about it
jokes.explanation: Perfect Roland inappropriateness - wanting to poeticize an awkward situation is exactly his character

jokes.text: Whoa! Aah! Stevie! Your friend just responded!
jokes.setup: Roland seeing Stevie's phone notification
jokes.punchline: His shocked reaction to presumably explicit response photo
jokes.explanation: Standard visual reaction gag, effective but not particularly creative

jokes.text: Yeah... I'd say her friend responded.
jokes.setup: Roland's shocked reaction to phone notification
jokes.punchline: Johnny's dry understatement about obvious situation
jokes.explanation: Good deadpan delivery from Johnny with economical punchline

jokes.text: That hot piece from the store?
jokes.setup: David mentioning Patrick has dinner with Ken
jokes.punchline: Alexis's inappropriate objectification
jokes.explanation: In-character for Alexis but fairly predictable objectification humor

jokes.text: Like, Pippa Middleton, or Rihanna.
jokes.setup: Alexis explaining her strategy of sending boyfriends with non-threats
jokes.punchline: Considering global celebrities as 'safe' options
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Alexis moment - casually name-dropping A-list celebrities as relationship non-threats reveals her delusional social status

jokes.text: Mm, tell that to his million dollar smile, David.
jokes.setup: David dismissing Ken as a threat
jokes.punchline: Alexis pointing out Ken's attractive features
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of David's anxiety with specific attractive feature mentioned

jokes.text: Josh Groban has a thick, leather-bound binder full of them.
jokes.setup: Discussing ground rules for open relationships
jokes.punchline: Casual reference to Josh Groban's relationship manual
jokes.explanation: Peak Alexis celebrity namedrop - the specificity of Josh Groban having relationship rules is perfectly absurd

jokes.text: If you'd've told me 20 years ago I'd be standing here with an empty chip bucket, stoned out of my mind at a Doodlebops concert, I would've lit a menthol, and had a laugh.
jokes.setup: Jocelyn reflecting on her current situation
jokes.punchline: Perfect summary of her decline with specific period details
jokes.explanation: Excellent character moment with great specificity - menthol cigarettes and Doodlebops perfectly capture her trajectory

jokes.text: Roland Jr.'s a huge fan of 'Wobbly Whoopsy.'
jokes.setup: Explaining why she went to Doodlebops concert
jokes.punchline: Specific ridiculous children's song title
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist children's entertainment name that sounds authentic and ridiculous

jokes.text: All except Charlie, but every party has a pooper.
jokes.setup: Moira claiming she 'rocked' with Rolling Stones
jokes.punchline: Calling Charlie Watts a party pooper
jokes.explanation: Great Moira celebrity story with specific band member knowledge - economical and character-perfect

jokes.text: we aging mortals are blessed with weakening eyes and memories so we don't have to really see ourselves.
jokes.setup: Jocelyn worried about aging
jokes.punchline: Moira's philosophical take on aging's benefits
jokes.explanation: Brilliant philosophical observation delivered in Moira's pretentious style - genuinely wise and funny

jokes.text: If you love the number 19, you go be 19.
jokes.setup: Jocelyn feeling 19 inside
jokes.punchline: Moira's empowering gambling advice
jokes.explanation: Sweet motivational moment that also works as gambling advice - economical and touching

jokes.text: Good taste in motels.
jokes.setup: Johnny approving of Emir
jokes.punchline: His objective business-focused compliment about romantic partner
jokes.explanation: Perfect Johnny business mindset applied to personal relationships - economical character comedy

jokes.text: No! Who knew everything would contain chicken? 'Black forest tenders.'
jokes.setup: Disappointing casino restaurant experience
jokes.punchline: Specific example of chicken in inappropriate dessert
jokes.explanation: Great specific example of terrible restaurant naming - economical and quotable

jokes.text: Hey Ted, I'll be there soon, but I just can't leave David alone right now. Yeah, no, apparently they left for dinner at seven. Radio silence.
jokes.setup: Alexis updating Ted on David's situation
jokes.punchline: Her dramatic summary of normal dinner timing
jokes.explanation: Alexis treating normal dinner as crisis situation - competent character comedy

jokes.text: something about his shoes just made me feel really weird, 'cause they were like, long and pointy, but then... Squared off at the toe. Yes!
jokes.setup: Patrick explaining why he couldn't go through with Ken date
jokes.punchline: Specific fashion critique of square-toed shoes
jokes.explanation: Perfect David/Patrick moment - specific fashion snobbery as relationship dealbreaker shows their compatibility

jokes.text: We all still like it, right?
jokes.setup: Jocelyn seeking validation for her drastic haircut
jokes.punchline: Her immediate insecurity after dramatic gesture
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Jocelyn's post-decision anxiety - relatable and in character

jokes.text: It's really short. ♪ Every rose has its thorn ♪
jokes.setup: Jocelyn asking for reassurance about haircut
jokes.punchline: Twyla's blunt honesty followed by Poison song reference
jokes.explanation: Perfect ending callback to Poison theme with Twyla's gentle honesty - economical and sweet
